title: Weakly supervised stop-motive experts with gated blending
summary: A single binary model mixes different mechanisms: degradation-driven stops, mandatory/finishability stops, wet-transition/legality stops, and competitive response stops.
rationale: A single binary model mixes different mechanisms: degradation-driven stops, mandatory/finishability stops, wet-transition/legality stops, and competitive response stops. Prior work and strategy literature suggest these motives behave differently. Separating them with weak labels is a concrete extension of generic stop-type heterogeneity.
implementation_hint: Create pseudo-cause labels from observables only: `cannot_finish`/service-window overage for degradation-driven, wet-dry compound transitions and stint-1 dry-race logic for legality/weather, and high field-relative pressure with feasible tyres for competitive response. Train 3 compact specialists plus a gating model that blends them from current-lap context; final prediction is the weighted sum.
expected_effect: Can improve calibration of extreme cases and recover signal currently blurred by one-size-fits-all thresholds.
risk: Pseudo-labels will be noisy and may encode your assumptions too strongly; keep experts simple and test whether they add ensemble diversity rather than standalone CV only.
